prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, matey! The sky be blue ’cause o’ a grand trick played by the very air itself. When the sun shines its golden beams down on our watery orb, the light be scatterin’ off tiny bits in the sky. The bluest rays, they be the quickest to scatter ‘bout all ways, paintin’ the heavens a fine blue for us landlubbers to spy. ’Tis the same reason the sea be blue too, for blue light be bouncin’ ’round more than the others. So, next time ye look up and see that vast blue above, remember it’s the air and light playin’ their piratical game, so we can marvel from the decks below! Arrr!
